name: Doomscroll
white: The read receipt, deployed as psychological warfare.
Leaving Mom on read for eleven days.
Replying "wrong chat" four seconds too late.
Doomscrolling in the fetal position until the birds start chirping.
The algorithm, which knew I was pregnant before I did.
Arguing with strangers as cardio.
An influencer apologizing in 4K with no makeup and a ring light.
A 45-second voice memo that could have been "ok."
The typing bubble appearing, vanishing, and reappearing for nine minutes.
The friend who replies "?" and nothing more.
Muting the group chat right as the drama gets good.
Main-character energy at a funeral.
"Seen 2:47 AM."
Posting through it.
An unboxing video of an empty box with 40 million views.
A true-crime podcast as a bedtime story.
The thirst trap that took forty minutes to look effortless.
Accidentally liking a photo from six years deep in an ex's page.
My screen time report, read aloud at my funeral.
The group chat that went silent the moment I joined.
The other group chat, the one without me in it.
Sending the screenshot to the person it was about.
A vertical video of a man screaming in his car.
The comment section, where hope goes to die.
A DM that just says "you up?" from a mattress brand.
Sixty slides of a stranger's wedding.
The apology video's apology video.
Unfollowing someone as a declaration of war.
Soft-launching my situationship.
Watching an ex's story at full brightness, wide awake, at 4 a.m.
The 2% of battery my phone saves for the worst possible moment.
A grown man doing a little dance for the algorithm.
Whispering "I'm so unwell" and opening the same app again.
The app I deleted, mourned, and reinstalled within the hour.
Checking who viewed my story like it's a crime scene.
A feed that knows me better than my therapist does.
A targeted ad for caskets.
Engagement bait from my own mother.
A gender reveal, live-streamed, on fire.
Pretending I didn't see the message I watched arrive in real time.
Replying to a story with the eggplant emoji and immediately losing my nerve.
A podcast with three guys, four microphones, and nothing to say.
An uncle going live from his recliner.
A fourteen-part story about the breakup.
The hard launch nobody asked for.
Two hundred unread messages in a chat named "quick question."
Falling asleep mid-scroll and getting slapped by my own phone.
The screen cracking directly across my ex's face.
A couple sharing one account, as a threat.
A thumbs-up reaction that ended a marriage.
The voice memo I re-recorded nine times to sound casual.
Posting into the void, and the void leaving one like.
A message request folder full of feral men.
A ring light in a filthy room.
A prank video that's just assault with a jump cut.
Narrating a stranger's public breakup to the group chat in real time.
Blocking my own mother, but just for the week.
A parasocial relationship with a woman who cleans fridges.
Accidentally heart-reacting to news of a death.
A month of silent lurking undone by one accidental like.
The engagement announcement I zoomed in on for twenty minutes.
Announcing my departure from the internet, then returning in nine hours.
A 3 a.m. deep dive into a stranger's divorce.
Nudes filed under "taxes 2019."
A sext torpedoed by autocorrect.
A dog with brand deals.
Two brand accounts flirting in public.
Doing it for the content.
A minion meme sent with complete sincerity.
Grandma commenting "WHO IS THIS" on my thirst trap.
A 40-minute video essay about a feud between two people I've never heard of.
The algorithm serving grief content on my birthday.
Refreshing the same three apps in rotation like a lab rat.
A neighborhood app three posts away from forming a militia.
The marketplace guy who asks "is this available" and is never seen again.
A boomer sharing a screenshot of a screenshot of a screenshot.
A "we need to talk" text sent right before my phone died.
The group chat holding a trial in my absence.
A gym bro filming strangers for content.
A "day in my life" video of a person doing nothing.

black.text: Wrong chat. WRONG CHAT. The thing about _ was meant for the other group chat.
It's 3 a.m. and I'm in the fetal position, scrolling through _.
The algorithm has decided my entire personality is _.
In today's tearful apology video, I finally address _.
Breaking news in the family group chat: Grandma has sent fourteen photos of _.
Day 47 of posting _ until my ex responds.
I got left on read after sending _.
Welcome back to my true-crime podcast. This week: _.
My screen time report says nine hours on _. And honestly? Worth it.
I muted the group chat for one hour and missed the entire meltdown about _.
She sent a three-minute voice memo just to describe _.
I'm not addicted to my phone. I could quit _ whenever I want.
Sorry for replying eight months late! Things got crazy with _.
Never read the comments, especially the ones about _.
Hey guys, welcome back to the channel! Today we're unboxing _.
He slid into my DMs with _.
Delete it. Delete it NOW. You cannot post _.
I woke up famous. A video of _ went viral overnight.
My phone heard me mention _ once, and now every ad is for _.
I logged on to argue about _ and somehow ended up defending _.
